subject: math
title: Introduction to Fractions
content:
Understanding Fractions

What is a Fraction?

A fraction represents a part of a whole. Think of cutting a pizza into equal slices — each slice is a fraction of the entire pizza!

Parts of a Fraction

A fraction has two numbers:

• The top number (numerator) tells us how many pieces we have
• The bottom number (denominator) tells us how many equal pieces the whole is divided into

For example, 3/8 means we have 3 pieces out of 8 total pieces.

Visual Fractions

Imagine a chocolate bar with 4 squares:
• Eating 1 square = 1/4 of the bar
• Eating 2 squares = 2/4 = 1/2 of the bar
• Eating 3 squares = 3/4 of the bar
• Eating all 4 squares = 4/4 = 1 (the whole bar!)

Proper Fractions

When the numerator is smaller than the denominator, we call this a proper fraction. These are always less than 1.

Examples: 1/2, 3/4, 2/5, 7/8

Practice: The pizza was cut into 6 slices. If you eat 2 slices, what fraction did you eat?
Answer: 2/6 which we can simplify to 1/3!

questions.prompt: What does the numerator in a fraction tell us?
choices: How many pieces the whole is divided into
The number on top of a fraction

questions.prompt: What fraction of the pizza is eaten if it has 8 slices and 3 are eaten?
questions.hint: First number is pieces eaten, second is total pieces
Fractions greater than 1/2 have numerator more than half the denominator
Divide both numbers by their greatest common factor (4)

questions.prompt: If a pizza is cut into 6 slices and all 6 are eaten, what fraction represents this?
questions.hint: When numerator equals denominator, the fraction equals 1
